title: manihot anomala subsp. pubescens
intro: Manihot anomala subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy manihot or hairy cassava, is a subspecies of the manihot anomala plant, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the hairy manihot plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the hairy manihot plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
